data.title: Hammack, lone Fed dissenter, sees risks skewed to inflation
data.description:
Cleveland Fed President Beth Hammack said stubborn inflation, combined with a strong labor market, led her to vote for keeping the Federal Reserve's policy rate unchanged on Wednesday. She was the lone Federal Open Market Committee member to vote against reducing the federal funds rate target range by 25 basis points to 4.25%-4.50%.

The balance of risks to the outlook appears to be skewed to higher inflation, she noted in a statement on Friday. That contrasts with the Fed's statement on Wednesday that risks appear to be "roughly balanced."

"In my mind, maintaining the target range for the federal funds rate at 4-1/2% to 4-3/4% at the December 2024 meeting was the best choice given the strength of recent economic data, accommodative financial conditions, and my forecast that inflation will remain somewhat above 2% over the next year amid a healthy labor market," Hammack said.

The economy is in a good position, but it's important to focus on inflation, she said. "Based on my estimate that monetary policy is not far from a neutral stance, I prefer to hold policy steady until we see further evidence that inflation is resuming its path to our 2% objective."

Her decision was a "close call," Hammack said, adding, "I appreciate the diverse perspectives that my FOMC colleagues brought to our robust discussion."

data.title: BioAtla announces registered direct offering to advance two mid-stage clinical programs, shares fall
data.description:
• BioAtla (NASDAQ:BCAB) said on Friday that it has entered into definitive agreements with certain institutional investors for the issuance and sale of 9.7M shares of its common stock in a registered direct offering.
  • Each share of common stock offered was sold with a warrant to purchase one share of common stock at an exercise price of $1.19 per share.
  • The combined offering price per share of common stock and accompanying warrant is $0.9520. The gross proceeds from the offering are expected to be approximately $9.2 million.
  • The company intends to use the net proceeds, along with its existing cash and cash equivalents, to fund its research and development efforts, including to reach several key inflection points for its mid-stage clinical T-Cell Engager (TCE) and Antibody Drug Conjugate (ADC) programs.
  • BioAtla (BCAB) shares were down more than 15% in pre-market trading.

data.title: The First of Long Island declares $0.21 dividend
data.description:
• The First of Long Island (NASDAQ:FLIC) declares $0.21/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 6.87%
  • Payable Jan. 9; for shareholders of record Dec. 31; ex-div Dec. 31.
  • See FLIC Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Clearwater Paper jumps after report of Suzano takeover interest
data.description:
Clearwater Paper (NYSE:CLW) soared 19% in premarket trading after a report that Brazil's Suzano SA (SUZ) is evaluating a takeover bid.

Suzano (SUZ) is working with an adviser as it tries to reach an agreement, according to a Bloomberg report late Thursday, which cited people familiar with the matter.

Suzano and Clearwater declined to comment to Bloomberg.

The potential Clearwater (CLW) deal comes after the Brazilian pulp and paper company dropped a $15 billion takeover offer for International Paper (IP) in June. Clearwater has a market cap of $409 million.

data.title: Golden Matrix resumes stock repurchase program
data.description:
• Golden Matrix Group (NASDAQ:GMGI) said on Thursday that it has resumed its existing stock repurchase program.
  • Under the repurchase program previously authorized by the board, the company may repurchase its outstanding shares of common stock from time to time in open market or privately-negotiated transactions, including accelerated share repurchase transactions, block trades, or pursuant to 10b5-1 trading plans.
  • The company has approximately $4.958 million available under the current program. The company currently has 129.24M shares of common stock outstanding.
  • Golden Matrix (GMGI) shares were up more than 6% in pre-market trading.

data.title: Metavesco announces 100-for-1 forward stock split
data.description:
• Metavesco (OTCPK:MVCO) announced on Friday the implementation of a 100-for-1 forward stock split of its common stock.
  • Shareholders of record as of the close of business on October 30, 2024, will have their shares reclassified such that each share of common stock will convert into 100 shares of common stock.
  • This adjustment will take effect after the close of trading on December 23, 2024, with the company’s common stock beginning to trade on a split-adjusted basis on December 24, 2024.
  • The stock split, along with an increase in the authorized number of shares of the company's common stock from 600 million shares to 15 billion shares, was approved by the company's board of directors and by the company's majority shareholder.

data.title: Stock index futures pare losses as core PCE comes softer than expected
data.description:
Stock index futures pared some losses on Friday, after the November's core PCE report came in cooler than expected.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) -0.8%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) -1% and Dow futures (INDU) -0.6%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) fell 2 basis points to 4.55%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) fell 6 basis points to 4.27%.

The Federal Reserve's favored inflation gauge - core PCE - ticked down in November, and edged up 0.1% M/M in November, less than the +0.2% consensus.

A stopgap spending plan backed by President-elect Donald Trump was rejected in the House of Representatives, further fueling concerns of a government shutdown.

"The big question now is whether a U.S. government shutdown is about to happen," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said.

The latest bill was voted down by 235 votes to 174. The funding is set to expire on Friday night.

"A short-lived shutdown affects government workers, but has limited economic impact. The longer a shutdown lasts, the more disruptive it is to the U.S. economy. Economic data may not get published in a shutdown," UBS' Paul Donovan said.

Wall Street's bounce back on Thursday following its worst session in over three months ran out of steam in the final hour of trading.

"For those wanting a quiet run up to Xmas, the good news is that there hasn't been any real follow-through to the Fed-induced slump on Wednesday. The bad news is that an initial recovery in markets struggled to gain traction yesterday," Reid added.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: BlackBerry rises as TD Cowen upgrades after Q3 results
data.description:
• BlackBerry (NYSE:BB) was in the spotlight on Friday after TD Cowen upgraded the enterprise software company following its third-quarter results and guidance.
  • Shares rose 1.4% in premarket trading.
  • "Results were better than expected," analyst Daniel Chan wrote in a note to clients. "While guidance and some KPIs suggest continued [secure communications] volatility, we cannot ignore the substantial improvement in the expected FCF generation from the entire business, which are likely helped by substantial [net operating losses] from Cylance."
  • Chan raised his rating on BlackBerry to Buy from Hold and raised his price target to $4 from $3.25.
  • Delving a bit deeper, Chan noted that BlackBerry is trading at 3.1 times forward revenue, "well below" its cybersecurity peers, which trade at 10.6 times and IoT peers, which trade at 4.9 times. "While a discount is likely warranted for the [secure communications] segment, given low growth and lackluster KPIs, we believe a 2.0 revenue multiple (to line up with our DCF) is fair," Chan added. "We continue to believe the IoT segment is deserving of a premium given its market dominance and highly defensible position."

data.title: Banzai acquires Vidello for up to $7M in a mix of cash equity
data.description:
• Banzai International (NASDAQ:BNZI) announced on Friday the acquisition of Vidello, a video hosting technology and marketing suite provider, for up to $7M in a mix of cash equity.
  • The marketing technology company, having signed a definitive agreement for the acquisition, was trading +17.90% pre-market at $1.91.
  • The acquisition is expected to grow Banzai's revenue by $6.5M and EBITDA by $2.3M for the twelve-month period ended September 30, 2024, on a pro-forma basis.
  • The aggregate merger consideration will be up to $5.5M in cash and a number of Banzai class A shares, and/or pre-funded warrants in lieu thereof, equal to $1.5M.
  • The transaction is expected to close this month.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Galectin drops as trial for lead asset fails in MASH
data.description:
Galectin Therapeutics (NASDAQ:GALT) lost ~25% in the premarket on Friday after its NAVIGATE clinical trial for lead candidate belapectin failed to reach the main goal in patients with liver disorder, Metabolic Dysfunction-Associated Steatohepatitis (MASH).

The global study, originally designed as a Phase 2b/3 trial, enrolled those with MASH (formerly known as NASH) cirrhosis who have clinical signs of portal hypertension and are at risk of developing esophageal varices.

Citing data from three patient cohorts who received belapectin 2 mg/kg, belapectin 4 mg/kg, or placebo, the company said the intent-to-treat population didn’t reach the primary goal of varices prevention with statistical significance.

However, in the per-protocol population, there was a statistically significant ~49% decline in the development of esophageal varices for the 2 mg/kg cohort compared to placebo, according to Galectin (NASDAQ:GALT).

The experimental therapy was well tolerated with no safety signals, and the rates of adverse events and serious adverse events were comparable across the three patient groups.

The company expects to determine the next steps in the program once additional data from about 50 patients who have undergone 36 months of belapectin therapy is available in early 2025.
